message:
perf(btree): chunks_exact+buf-reuse for cell pointer parse (OPT-4)

Profile showed read_cell_pointers 7.84% + CellRef::parse 3.81% self-time
on INSERT hot path. Three orthogonal fixes:

1. read_cell_pointers now uses chunks_exact(2) on the already-bounds-
   checked pointer-array slice. The explicit per-index loop was doing
   page[off] / page[off + 1] with a bounds check on each of the two
   bytes (plus an add on every iteration). chunks_exact(2).map(u16::
   from_be_bytes) hoists the bounds check out to the slice creation
   and lets LLVM autovectorize the big-endian decode.

2. Added read_cell_pointers_into(&mut Vec<u16>) — a public variant
   that lets hot callers own their buffer and reuse its allocation
   across pages. The old function becomes a thin wrapper. This opens
   the door for cursor.rs / balance.rs to drop the per-page Vec
   allocation in a follow-up without breaking the existing API.

3. Removed two tracing::debug!("decoded btree cell boundary", ...)
   calls from CellRef::parse. tracing debug! is not compile-time
   stripped (no max_level_* feature in workspace deps), so every
   cell parse paid the dispatcher-lookup cost even with no
   subscriber registered. Per-cell × 100+ cells × every INSERT adds
   up to measurable overhead on the hot path and these logs were
   never useful for debugging at that volume.

Tests: all 24 cell::tests pass; no new fsqlite-btree regressions
(pre-existing test failures in cursor::tests are unchanged and
unrelated to this change).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
